info.title: Jupico - OpenAPI 3.0
info.description: The Jupico API lets Service Providers onboard merchants, process card and eCheck (ACH) payments, run hosted payment pages, manage recurring billing, send payouts, and query every resource. All endpoints use HTTPS with Basic authentication and accept and return JSON. Endpoints are grouped by workflow: onboard merchants first, then accept payments, then move funds and report.

tags.name: Status
tags.description: Health check for the Jupico API.

tags.name: Onboarding Invites
tags.description: Create and send invites that onboard merchants through the Jupico-hosted flow, with optional pre-filled identity, fees, and payout bank account data.

tags.name: Provisioning
tags.description: Onboard merchants from your own UI. Collect onboarding data and submit it through the provisioning chain: applicant → application → submerchant.

tags.name: Session
tags.description: Create browser authorization sessions for the Jupico web components, so card and bank account data is tokenized client-side and never touches your servers.

tags.name: Tokenization
tags.description: Convert one-time tokens from the web components into permanent Jupico tokens, or tokenize raw card data if your systems are PCI DSS compliant.

tags.name: Customer
tags.description: Create and manage customer records that payments, subscriptions, installments, and invoices link to.

tags.name: Card Transactions
tags.description: Process credit and debit card transactions using tokenized card data: sale, authorization, capture, void, refund, and rollback.

tags.name: eCheck Transactions
tags.description: Debit customer bank accounts via eCheck (ACH): sale, void, refund, rollback, and bank account tokenization.

tags.name: Checkouts
tags.description: Hosted checkout sessions where customers complete payment on a Jupico-hosted page.

tags.name: Payment Links
tags.description: Shareable links that open a Jupico-hosted payment page — no integration code needed at the point of sharing.

tags.name: Invoices
tags.description: Create and email invoices that customers pay online.

tags.name: Subscriptions
tags.description: Recurring billing. Plans define billing schedules and pricing models; subscriptions enroll customers against a stored payment token.

tags.name: Installments
tags.description: Split a fixed total into scheduled payments with an optional deposit.

tags.name: Payouts
tags.description: Send funds from a submerchant's available balance to a tokenized bank account with on-demand payout instructions.

tags.name: Query
tags.description: Search and report across every resource: transactions, merchants, settlements, balances, disputes, fees, and more. All query endpoints accept the same filter, sort, and pagination envelope — see the Query Capabilities guide.

get.description: Returns `200` when the API is reachable. Use this endpoint for health checks and uptime monitoring; it requires no request body.

post.description: Creates an onboarding invite without sending it. Pre-fill identity, fees, and payout bank account data to shorten the hosted onboarding flow for the merchant. Send the invite later with **Send an invite**.

name.description: The name of the person being invited.

email.description: The email of the person being invited.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the person being invited.

identity.description: The identity object containing data about the business and its representatives

payoutBankAccounts.description: Bank accounts where the merchant receives payouts.

feesTemplateId.description: UUID of the fees template with predefined fees. If no value is passed, the id of the default template will be used.

fees.description: The fees object will overwrite the values set on the fees template, as specified by the feesTemplateId. This allows for customization of fees at the invite overriding any defaults defined in the template.

Creates a customer record that payments, subscriptions, installments, and invoices can be linked to. Returns the created customer.
Updates an existing customer's details. Returns the updated customer.
Deletes a customer record. Returns the deleted customer.

A sale combines authorization and capture in one transaction. Use it when purchases are fulfilled immediately, such as retail or e-commerce checkout. If the issuing bank approves, an approval code is returned; if it declines, the response includes the decline reason.

An authorization confirms the cardholder's ability to pay and reserves the amount without charging it. Use it when the final amount is unknown or fulfillment is deferred — for example, tips at a restaurant or a card on file at a hotel. Complete the charge later with **Capture**.

A capture confirms a previous authorization and initiates settlement, charging the customer. The capture amount may exceed the authorized amount up to an allowable excess limit determined by the issuing bank.
Cancels a transaction that has not yet settled, so the customer is never charged. Once a transaction has settled, use **Refund** instead.
Returns funds to the customer's card for a previously settled transaction. Use refunds for returns, cancellations, or to resolve disputes. For unsettled transactions, use **Void** instead.

A rollback (communications/system reversal) cancels a transaction whose response you never received — for example, after a timeout. As the original requestor, you are responsible for generating a rollback for every transaction that does not receive a response, to prevent duplicate or inconsistent charges.

Initiates a direct debit (ACH) from the customer's bank account. If the debit request is accepted, the transaction proceeds to settlement; otherwise the response includes the failure reason. Note that ACH returns can occur days after acceptance.
Cancels an eCheck transaction that has not yet been submitted for settlement, so no debit occurs. Once settled, use **Refund** instead.
Returns funds to the customer's bank account for a previously settled eCheck debit. Use refunds for returns, cancellations, or to resolve disputes.

A rollback (communications/system reversal) cancels an eCheck transaction whose response was not reliably delivered. Generate a rollback for any eCheck transaction that does not receive a response, to prevent duplicate or inconsistent charges. To return funds for a settled transaction, use **Refund** instead.
Tokenizes a customer's bank account details. Use the returned token in place of raw account data for eCheck sales and recurring payments.

post.description: Creates an installment plan that splits a total amount into scheduled payments for a customer.
